lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.title: Metro 2039 Release Date
lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.intro: Metro 2039 is officially announced, and the public timeline is defined by the announcement beat, the full reveal date, and a launch window that is still listed as TBA.

steps.title: Metro 2039 Official Status
steps.description: Metro 2039 is announced as the next core Metro chapter, giving the reveal cycle a clear official starting point.

steps.title: Metro 2039 Announcement Date
steps.description: The public announcement opened on April 13, 2026 and set the stage for the first full reveal.

steps.title: Metro 2039 Full Reveal Date
steps.description: The first full look is scheduled for April 16, 2026 as the biggest beat in the current rollout.

steps.title: Metro 2039 Premiere Time
steps.description: The livestream window is 10:00 AM PT / 1:00 PM ET / 6:00 PM UK / 7:00 PM CEST / 8:00 PM EET.

steps.title: Metro 2039 Launch Window
steps.description: Metro 2039 still carries a TBA release date while the reveal campaign moves from teaser to full showcase.

quickTips: Metro 2039 is positioned as the fourth mainline Metro game.
The April 16 reveal is the most important calendar marker on the homepage.
The official site and Xbox First Look stream are the two primary release-date tracking points.
The launch date stays TBA until Deep Silver and 4A Games publish the next release update.

lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.title: Metro 2039 Trailer
lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.intro: The Metro 2039 video rollout already has a teaser, a scheduled Xbox First Look premiere, and two official watch hubs that anchor the reveal cycle.

cards.name: Metro 2039 Announcement Teaser
cards.description: The first Metro 2039 teaser is the cleanest starting point for the reveal slate and the fastest way to catch the game's opening tone.

cards.name: Metro 2039 Xbox First Look
cards.description: The full reveal stream with 4A Games and Deep Silver is the main event for the next wave of Metro 2039 footage.

cards.name: Metro 2039 Official Game Hub
cards.description: The Deep Silver page is the central official landing page for the video rollout, reminders, and reveal updates.

cards.name: Metro 2039 Official YouTube Feed
cards.description: The Metro channel is the fastest place to track teaser uploads, premiere links, and future trailer drops.

milestones: Set a reminder for the Metro 2039 full reveal

lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.title: Metro 2039 Story
lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.intro: The Metro 2039 story picture already has five clear anchors: series position, post-apocalyptic setting, war-shaped tone, political DNA, and Dmitry Glukhovsky's continuing role.

items.name: Metro 2039 Continues the Mainline Saga
items.description: Metro 2039 is framed as the next mainline entry after Metro 2033, Metro: Last Light, and Metro Exodus.

items.name: Metro 2039 Keeps the Moscow Survival Lens
items.description: The new chapter still sits inside Metro's post-apocalyptic world of survivors, tunnels, surface danger, and hard political choices.

items.name: Metro 2039 Carries a War-Shaped Tone
items.description: 4A Games has tied the next Metro story to the lived pressure of war, freedom, repression, and human cost.

items.name: Metro 2039 Stays Political and Emotional
items.description: The studio keeps Metro's anti-war and anti-tyranny identity at the center of the new narrative.

items.name: Metro 2039 Still Includes Dmitry Glukhovsky
items.description: Dmitry Glukhovsky remains part of the franchise direction, keeping the book-and-game connection active in Metro 2039.

lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.title: Metro 2039 Gameplay
lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.intro: Metro 2039 gameplay is already easy to frame on the homepage: it is a shooter built around combat, stealth, survival pressure, exploration, and a story-driven single-player focus.

solutions.name: Metro 2039 Shooter Foundation
solutions.description: Metro 2039 is being tracked as a shooter, which keeps it aligned with the franchise's first-person identity.

solutions.name: Metro 2039 Combat and Stealth
solutions.description: Combat and stealth remain the clearest gameplay pillar for Metro 2039 because they define the official Metro formula.

solutions.name: Metro 2039 Exploration and Survival
solutions.description: Resource pressure, hostile terrain, and careful scavenging continue to shape how Metro 2039 is expected to play.

solutions.name: Metro 2039 Story-Driven Single Player
solutions.description: 4A Games keeps the next Metro centered on a crafted single-player campaign rather than live-service structure.

solutions.name: Metro 2039 Gen 9 and PC Tech Baseline
solutions.description: Metro 2039 is built for current-generation console hardware and PC, matching the franchise's push toward advanced rendering.

managementTips: Expect Metro 2039 to blend stealth, gunplay, and pressure rather than chase arcade spectacle.
Use Metro Exodus as the best public reference point for Metro 2039 gameplay DNA.
Treat resource management and environmental hazards as part of the core loop.
Watch the first full reveal for the first real Metro 2039 combat and traversal read.

lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.title: Metro 2039 Latest News
lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.subtitle: Track the official reveal rollout, major press coverage, and the community pulse in one place.
lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.intro: The public Metro 2039 news cycle is centered on a short but high-impact reveal window. This feed gives visitors one place to follow what changed, when it changed, and where the next official update will land.

items.headline: Metro 2039 official teaser page is live
items.summary: Deep Silver launched the Metro 2039 site, opened reminder-email signup, and set the reveal for Apr 16, 2026 at 10 AM PDT / 7 PM CEST / 8 PM EET.

items.headline: Metro 2039 Xbox First Look becomes the world-premiere broadcast
items.summary: Xbox announced a digital-only Metro 2039 reveal with 4A Games and Deep Silver and framed it as the fourth mainline Metro game after Metro 2033, Metro: Last Light, and Metro Exodus.

items.headline: Metro 2039 press coverage is focused on what follows Exodus
items.summary: Early coverage centers on what comes next after Metro Exodus, especially for Artyom, the Spartans, and the wider world beyond Moscow's tunnels.

items.headline: Metro 2039 community discussion is clustering around the countdown
items.summary: The main Metro subreddit is acting as the fastest-moving fan hub for reveal chatter, leak retrospectives, and reaction threads ahead of the Apr 16 broadcast.

lucidBlocksWorldRegions.title: Metro 2039 First Look Livestream Guide
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.subtitle: Everything needed for the Apr 16 first look, from start time and watch links to subtitles, accessibility, and post-show recap coverage.
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.intro: This module should be the fastest route from search intent to watch intent. It covers the exact air time, where to watch, what language support is available, and where visitors should go once the live premiere ends.

steps.title: Mark the exact Metro 2039 broadcast time
steps.detail: Xbox First Look: Metro 2039 begins on Thursday, Apr 16, 2026 at 10:00 AM Pacific / 1:00 PM Eastern / 6:00 PM UK.

steps.title: Watch Metro 2039 on the Xbox YouTube premiere
steps.detail: The official live destination is the Xbox YouTube channel, and the Metro 2039 site also points visitors to the Xbox reminder page.

steps.title: Use Metro 2039 subtitles if you are not watching in English
steps.detail: Xbox confirmed subtitle support for Arabic, Brazilian Portuguese, Bulgarian, Croatian, Czech, Danish, Dutch, Finnish, French, German, Greek, Hungarian, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Norwegian, Polish, Romanian, Slovakian, Spanish, Swedish, Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, Turkish, and Ukrainian.

steps.title: Check Metro 2039 accessibility options before the show starts
steps.detail: Xbox says the event will also have an English audio-described version and an American Sign Language version on Xbox's YouTube channel.

steps.title: Use the official Metro 2039 recap after the premiere ends
steps.detail: Xbox Wire says it will publish a full recap immediately after the show, including localized recap coverage in Brazilian Portuguese, French, German, LATAM Spanish, and Japanese.

lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.title: Metro 2039 Platform Status
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.subtitle: A clean snapshot of where Metro 2039 is publicly surfacing right now across PC, Steam, Xbox, and PlayStation.
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.intro: Public platform information is still early, so this table focuses on what has actually surfaced: where the game has been listed, which storefronts are live as watch hubs, and which purchase pages have not gone public yet.

rows.currentStatus: Present in early media platform listings
rows.publicStorefront: No standalone PC purchase page is linked in the current announcement materials
rows.editionStatus: No edition breakdown is public yet
rows.notes: GameSpot lists Metro 2039 for PC, while the official push is still centered on the reveal stream rather than a buy page.

rows.currentStatus: No public Metro 2039 app page has surfaced yet
rows.publicStorefront: Steam is currently surfacing Metro franchise news about the showcase instead of a Metro 2039 product page
rows.editionStatus: No preorder or package details are public yet
rows.notes: The easiest Steam-facing public entry right now is the showcase news post attached to existing Metro products, not a Metro 2039 store listing.

rows.currentStatus: Primary reveal partner and official watch destination
rows.publicStorefront: The public announcement flow is centered on Xbox Wire and the Xbox YouTube premiere
rows.editionStatus: No edition matrix is public yet
rows.notes: Xbox is the clearest official entry point for console players right now because the Apr 16 first look is being hosted through Xbox channels.

rows.currentStatus: Present in early media platform listings
rows.publicStorefront: No public PlayStation Store page is linked in the current announcement wave
rows.editionStatus: No version or preorder details are public yet
rows.notes: PS5 is already showing up in early coverage, but storefront and package information have not been rolled out publicly.

lucidBlocksMobilityGear.title: Metro 2039 Characters and Returning Faces
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.subtitle: The public reveal has not named Metro 2039-exclusive characters yet, so this hub starts with the core Metro figures most likely to anchor early search demand after the reveal.
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.intro: Character intent is one of the easiest ways to grow internal links once the reveal expands. Until the Apr 16 showcase names the new cast, this module leads with the legacy figures players already associate with the mainline Metro story.

items.name: Current Metro 2039 reveal status
items.status: No new character names are public yet
items.summary: The official Metro 2039 page and Xbox First Look announcement confirm the game and the reveal date, but they do not yet publish new character names.

items.name: Artyom
items.summary: Deep Silver describes Artyom as a Metro-born survivor who spent his life protecting the tunnels while trying to prove that life exists beyond them.

items.name: Anna
items.summary: Anna is Miller's daughter, Artyom's wife, and one of the defining voices of the Aurora crew, making her essential context for Metro 2039 readers.

items.name: Miller
items.summary: Colonel Miller is the commanding figure behind the Spartan Rangers' chain of command and the Aurora expedition, shaping the military tone of the series.

lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.title: Metro 2039 Story Recap
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.subtitle: Metro 2039 story recap should be the fastest way to understand the three core Artyom games before the next chapter.
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.intro: For most visitors, the clean Metro 2039 catch-up path is the core Artyom trilogy. Metro 2033 establishes underground survival, Metro: Last Light turns that world into a political crisis, and Metro Exodus pushes the saga beyond Moscow.

items.title: Metro 2033
items.summary: After the 2013 nuclear war, Artyom grows up inside the Moscow Metro, humanity's largest refuge underground. When a new threat puts the network at risk, he leaves home and crosses stations, tunnels, and the poisoned surface to warn the rest of mankind.
keyPoints: Establishes the Moscow Metro as the center of the Metro 2039 backstory
Introduces Artyom as the main hero of the core game line
Sets the survival-horror tone of scarce resources, dark tunnels, and surface danger
items.bridge: Metro 2033 is the foundation chapter for Metro 2039 readers. It explains who Artyom is, how the Metro world works, and why later chapters keep revisiting the limits of underground survival.

items.title: Metro: Last Light
items.summary: One year later, the Metro faces pressure from outside threats and internal power struggles. Artyom moves through station-cities fighting over D6, a military stockpile powerful enough to decide the political future of underground civilization.
keyPoints: Expands Metro from local survival into a conflict over power and ideology
Raises the stakes with D6 and the risk of Metro-scale civil war
Keeps Artyom central while widening the political scope of the world
items.bridge: Metro: Last Light is the key middle chapter before Metro 2039. It carries the emotional and political setup that the next mainline era depends on.

items.title: Metro Exodus
items.summary: A quarter-century after nuclear war, Artyom leaves the Moscow Metro with Anna and the Spartan Rangers aboard the Aurora. The story becomes a year-long journey across post-apocalyptic Russia in search of a future beyond the tunnels.
keyPoints: Moves Metro beyond Moscow into a broader Russian wasteland
Follows Artyom and the Spartans through a full seasonal journey
Creates the clearest mainline setup currently available before Metro 2039
items.bridge: If Metro 2033 is about surviving underground and Last Light is about defending that world, Exodus is about deciding whether Metro life should be left behind. That question leads directly into Metro 2039 interest.

lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.title: Metro 2039 Series Timeline
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.subtitle: Use the Metro 2039 timeline to scan the story-world order from the Last War to Metro Exodus in one pass.
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.intro: This Metro 2039 timeline follows story year instead of release year. It helps readers place the nuclear war, prequel-era material, Artyom's three major game eras, and the strongest bridge period before the next mainline chapter.

items.title: The Last War devastates the surface
items.description: Nuclear war destroys normal life, poisons the surface, and pushes Moscow survivors underground into the Metro. Every later Metro book and game grows out of this break with the old world.

items.title: Metro Awakening becomes the earliest playable chapter
items.description: Set 15 years after nuclear fire, Metro Awakening follows Serdar through the tunnels in a VR-only story. It expands the franchise backward in time before Artyom's core arc.

items.title: Artyom's first journey defines the game saga
items.description: By 2033, an entire generation has been born underground. Artyom leaves the station-cities to warn humanity about a growing threat and becomes the central figure of the Metro game line.

items.title: The saga splits into major book and game threads
items.description: The books widen through Homer, Sasha, and Hunter during a deadly outbreak, while the games keep Artyom central as Metro politics escalate toward conflict over D6.

items.title: Artyom searches for life beyond the tunnels
items.description: Artyom keeps risking radiation to scan for other survivors and uncovers truths that reshape his understanding of Metro life. This is the strongest book bridge into the wider future.

items.title: The series leaves Moscow behind
items.description: Metro Exodus sends Artyom and the Spartan Rangers across Russia aboard the Aurora through a story that spans the seasons. The franchise expands from tunnel survival into a broader wasteland journey.

lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.title: Metro 2039 Game Order
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.subtitle: The clean Metro 2039 prep route is the main trilogy first, with Metro Awakening as optional VR context.
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.intro: If your goal is Metro 2039 readiness without confusion, follow the Artyom mainline path first. Metro 2033 Redux, Metro: Last Light Redux, and Metro Exodus are the core run; Metro Awakening adds bonus context if you also want the earlier VR prequel.

items.title: Start with Metro 2033 Redux
items.description: This is the definitive modern version of the first Metro game and the best entry point for new players. It introduces Artyom, station-cities, surface danger, and the survival rules of the universe.
details: Steam release date: August 25, 2014
Definitive modern version of the first mainline story
Best first stop before any other Metro game

items.title: Continue with Metro: Last Light Redux
items.description: Last Light follows Artyom in 2034 and carries the political and character setup forward from Metro 2033. It is the chapter that turns Metro into a conflict over factions, power, and the future of the underground world.
details: Steam release date: August 25, 2014
Direct narrative follow-up to Metro 2033
Redux package includes all previously released DLC

items.title: Then play Metro Exodus
items.description: Exodus is the third mainline game and the moment the series leaves Moscow behind. It pays off Artyom's earlier arc and remains the clearest setup currently available before Metro 2039.
details: Steam release date: February 14, 2019
Story year: 2036
Follows a continent-spanning journey aboard the Aurora

items.title: Treat Metro Awakening as an optional VR prequel
items.description: Metro Awakening is a VR-only story set in 2028, earlier than the Artyom trilogy. It adds useful prequel context, but the core non-VR catch-up route still runs through 2033 Redux, Last Light Redux, and Exodus.
details: Steam release date: November 7, 2024
VR-only Metro entry
Best used as bonus context if you own VR hardware or want deeper prequel lore

lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.title: Metro 2039 Books Order
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.subtitle: The clean Metro 2039 reading route is Metro 2033, then Metro 2034, then Metro 2035.
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.intro: For most readers preparing for Metro 2039, the key question is both order and relevance to the games. The core trilogy reads best in numbered sequence: Metro 2033 builds the foundation, Metro 2034 widens the cast, and Metro 2035 brings Artyom back while pulling major threads together.

items.title: Read Metro 2033 first
items.description: Metro 2033 is the opening chapter of the core Metro novels and the clearest way to learn the rules, politics, and mood of the Moscow Metro. It is also the novel with the strongest overlap to the first game.
details: Core Metro novel centered on Artyom
Establishes the underground world that later books and games build on
Best single starting point for both readers and players

items.title: Read Metro 2034 second
items.description: Metro 2034 moves one year forward but shifts away from Artyom. It expands the saga through Sasha, Homer, and Hunter and acts as the bridge from a single-hero story to a wider Metro world.
details: Story year: 2034
Expands Metro beyond Artyom's viewpoint
Best read before Metro 2035 because its characters carry forward

items.title: Finish with Metro 2035
items.description: Metro 2035 returns Artyom to center stage and pushes him toward the question of life beyond Moscow. It works as the main convergence point between long-form novel themes and game-era discussion before Metro 2039.
details: Story year: 2035
Returns focus to Artyom
Strongest final core read for users moving between books and games

lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.title: Metro 2039 Factions
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.subtitle: Metro politics is built around stations, supply lines, and armed blocs. These are the factions that give the series its long-running political identity.
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.intro: For Metro 2039, factions are not background lore. They explain who controls trade, who controls violence, who protects knowledge, and why any return to Moscow politics immediately becomes a fight over lines, stations, and survival.

items.title: Hanza
items.summary: The Hanseatic League is the Metro's commercial superpower and the closest thing the tunnels have to an economic center.
details: Controls the Koltsevaya Ring Line, the key trade route of the Metro.
Trade from every station and faction passes through Hanza checkpoints.
Maintains roughly 4,000 to 5,000 soldiers and protects commerce more than ideology.

items.title: Polis
items.summary: Polis is the intellectual heart of the Metro and one of the few places where pre-war culture and learning still feel alive.
details: Centered on the Arbatskaya, Alexandrovsky Sad, Biblioteka Imeni Lenina, and Borovitskaya transfer point.
Ruled by a council rather than a single head of state.
Protected both by its small military force and by its status as the Metro's repository of knowledge.

items.title: Spartan Order
items.summary: The Spartans are the Metro's most respected military order and Artyom's core faction.
details: An elite paramilitary force closely tied to Polis.
Occupies the Sparta surface base and D6 after the events of Metro 2033.
Has about 250 members, with roughly 100 in the field at any given time.

items.title: Red Line
items.summary: The Red Line is a Marxist-Leninist, neo-Stalinist state built on territory, manpower, and control.
details: Led by General Secretary Maxim Moskvin.
Controls most of the Sokolnicheskaya Line.
Has around 15,000 citizens, with about 6,000 to 7,000 serving in its armed forces.

items.title: Fourth Reich
items.summary: The Reich is Metro's fascist bloc, defined by xenophobia, militarization, and constant conflict with the Red Line.
details: Built around neo-Nazi and pre-war xenophobic ideology.
Centered on the Tverskaya-Chekhovskaya-Pushkinskaya station complex.
Fields an army of roughly 2,000 to 3,000 and stays in near-permanent struggle with the Red Line.

items.title: Invisible Watchers
items.summary: Metro 2035 reveals a hidden layer of power above the visible factions: the remnants of the old Russian state.
details: Presented as hidden survivors of the Russian government.
Keep major faction leaders in line through threats, blackmail, and bribes.
Use controlled conflict and managed instability as tools of population control.

lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.title: Metro 2039 Setting Explained
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.subtitle: New players do not need every lore detail first. They need the rules of the world: why people live underground, why masks matter, and why every journey feels like survival horror.
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.intro: Metro is not just a post-apocalyptic backdrop. It is a system with clear survival rules: underground shelter, poisoned surface air, station politics, dangerous travel, and one central question that keeps pushing the series forward - whether humanity is meant to stay in the tunnels at all.

items.heading: Why people live in the Metro
items.summary: The tunnels are humanity's last functioning refuge.
items.content: After the Last War devastated the world, only forty thousand people made it and took refuge in the Moscow Metro. Two decades later, the surface is still poisoned by nuclear fallout and mutant life, so the tunnels have become a full underground civilization.
points: The Metro was designed to function as a giant air-raid shelter.
Stations evolved into homes, markets, and political communities.
The entire series starts from the idea that survival moved underground.

items.heading: Why gas masks matter
items.summary: Masks are a survival rule, not just an iconic visual.
items.content: On the surface of Moscow, dangerous radiation makes gas masks mandatory. In Metro, every trip above ground is limited by air, filters, and exposure, which turns exploration into a timed risk.
points: The surface of Moscow is cloaked in dangerous radiation.
Artyom and the Rangers wear gas masks at all times on the surface.
Exposure above ground is one of the world's clearest danger systems.

items.heading: How stations become city-states
items.summary: The Metro is a patchwork of local powers rather than one unified society.
items.content: Each station develops its own leader, customs, and political identity. That is why the Metro feels less like one nation and more like a map of miniature states connected by tunnels, trade, fear, and armed borders.
points: Every station has its own rules and internal culture.
Trade and travel link communities that do not share the same values.
Politics in the tunnels matter as much as monsters on the surface.

items.heading: What makes travel deadly
items.summary: Moving between places is dangerous even when no war is happening.
items.content: The tunnels are never safe transit routes. Radiation pockets, mental threats, bandits, and mutant predators make even ordinary movement a survival act, which is why guides, routes, and faction territory matter so much in Metro discussions.
points: Radiation is not limited to the open surface.
Bandits and predators prey on travelers between stations.
Mental and supernatural-feeling threats are part of the series' atmosphere.

items.heading: What the surface adds beyond Moscow
items.summary: Exodus widens the world without making it safer.
items.content: Once the series moves beyond Moscow, Metro becomes a broader survival journey across Russia. Exodus adds anomalies, sandstorms, wildlife, and resource scarcity to the old Metro formula, showing that leaving the tunnels changes the world but not the danger.
points: Anomalies are dangerous electric spheres that follow repeatable paths.
Sandstorms can reduce visibility and turn open ground into a death trap.
Ammunition and supplies stay scarce even outside the Metro.

items.heading: Why Artyom keeps looking outward
items.summary: Artyom's defining belief is that life cannot end in the tunnels.
items.content: In Metro 2035, Artyom keeps venturing above ground to scan the airwaves and search for other survivors. In Exodus, that belief becomes a journey across Russia, making the search for a livable future one of the strongest narrative bridges into Metro 2039.
points: Artyom wants to take survivors back to the surface and find a new home.
He repeatedly risks radiation exposure to prove there is life beyond Moscow.
This outward drive is one of the core ideas that carries the series forward.

lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.title: Metro 2039 Endings Explained
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.subtitle: Metro endings are not bonus trivia. They shape how players understand Artyom, the Dark Ones, D6, Miller, and the future beyond Moscow.
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.intro: Putting Metro 2033, Metro Last Light, and Metro Exodus side by side gives users a clean canon map before they dive into Metro 2039. This is the fastest way to understand which outcomes define the series and which legacy questions still matter most.

items.heading: Metro 2033: Ranger vs Enlightened
items.summary: The first major branch decides the fate of the Dark Ones.
items.content: Metro 2033 has two endings. The canonical Ranger ending lets the missile strike destroy the Dark Ones. The alternate Enlightened ending appears if Artyom earns enough moral points and destroys the guidance system instead, realizing the Dark Ones wanted peace.
points: Canonical path: Ranger
Alternate path: Enlightened
Core consequence: the Dark Ones are destroyed or spared

items.heading: Metro Last Light: C'est la Vie vs Redemption
items.summary: Last Light continues from the canonical outcome of Metro 2033 and then creates its own major branch.
items.content: Metro Last Light begins after the path where Artyom destroyed the Dark Ones and joined the Rangers at D6. Its two endings are C'est la Vie, where Artyom detonates D6, and Redemption, where the Baby Dark One and the other Dark Ones save D6. Metro 2035 and Metro Exodus follow the Redemption line.
points: Canonical path: Redemption
C'est la Vie ends with D6 destroyed
Redemption preserves D6 and the Rangers' forward continuity

items.heading: Metro Exodus: Your Destination vs Eternal Voyage
items.summary: Exodus turns the ending question into Artyom's survival after the Novosibirsk mission.
items.content: Exodus splits between Your Destination and Eternal Voyage. In Your Destination, Artyom lives, Anna is saved, and he becomes the new leader of both the Order and the new community at Lake Baikal. In Eternal Voyage, Anna is still saved, but Artyom dies of radiation poisoning after securing the cure.
points: Your Destination: Artyom lives
Eternal Voyage: Artyom dies
Both endings revolve around the run for Anna's cure and the cost paid in Novosibirsk

items.heading: The legacy Metro 2039 inherits
items.summary: The trilogy leaves three major inheritance lines for the next discussion.
items.content: Taken together, the trilogy hands Metro 2039 three enduring legacy threads: the memory of the Dark Ones, the survival of D6 and the Spartan Order, and Artyom's fate after Lake Baikal. These are the questions players carry into every new Metro reveal, theory, and story recap.
points: Metro 2033 frames the Dark Ones question
Last Light decides whether the Rangers keep D6 and a viable future
Exodus turns the biggest remaining branch into Artyom's survival and leadership

lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.title: Metro 2039 Developer Explained
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.subtitle: Search interest around Metro 2039 is already spilling into questions about 4A Games, Deep Silver, Dmitry Glukhovsky, and the studio's structure. This module gives users a clean answer to who is making the game and how that team is organized.
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.intro: A lot of Metro search traffic now includes developer-side questions: Is 4A still the real Metro team, what is Deep Silver's role, where does Dmitry fit in, and how does Reburn connect to the old 4A setup. This module answers those directly from the current official pages and 4A's 2025 updates.

items.title: Current Official Status
items.summary: Metro 2039 already has a live official page and a scheduled showcase.
details: Deep Silver's official Metro 2039 page is online now.
The page lists the reveal for April 16, 2026 at 10AM PDT / 7PM CEST / 8PM EET.
It also points users to the official Xbox YouTube channel for event notifications.

items.title: Publisher, Developer, Creator
items.summary: The official credits answer the simplest question first: who publishes it, who develops it, and what it is based on.
details: Published by Deep Silver, a division of PLAION.
Developed by 4A Games.
Based on Dmitry Glukhovsky's Metro book trilogy.

items.title: Same Metro Team, Not a Rebrand
items.summary: 4A's February 2025 update was written to clear up confusion around the Metro team.
details: 4A says it is the team that created and built the Metro games.
The studio says it is still making the next Metro with Dmitry Glukhovsky.
4A explicitly describes itself as the same core founders, leadership, and developers rather than a renamed replacement.

items.title: Where 4A Works Today
items.summary: Metro 2039 is being built by a two-country studio with Ukraine still at its center.
details: 4A says it now has 200+ staff across two countries.
Roughly 150 of those developers remain in Ukraine.
The team works from Kyiv, Sliema in Malta, and remote locations.

items.title: How 4A and Reburn Connect
items.summary: The old 4A naming split is one of the biggest sources of online confusion, and 4A addressed it directly.
details: In 2014, the original founders formed 4A Games Limited and created a Malta headquarters.
That Malta-led team continued working closely with 4A Games Ukraine on Metro Exodus.
After Exodus, 4A Games Ukraine pursued its own project and later renamed itself Reburn, while 4A Games Limited continued Metro.

items.title: What Shapes the Next Metro
items.summary: 4A's March 2025 anniversary update explains why the next Metro sounds darker and more politically charged.
details: 4A says the full-scale invasion in 2022 changed how it wanted to tell the next Metro story.
The studio says the next game is being shaped by lived wartime experience.
4A also says Metro's anti-war, political, and emotional themes continue through its collaboration with Dmitry Glukhovsky.

faq.title: Metro 2039
faq.subtitle: Fast answers for the most common Metro 2039 questions

questions.question: When is Metro 2039 releasing?
questions.answer: Metro 2039 is announced, the full reveal is set for April 16, 2026, and the final release date is still TBA.

questions.question: Where can I watch the Metro 2039 reveal?
questions.answer: Watch the Metro 2039 reveal through the Xbox First Look YouTube premiere and the official Deep Silver game page.

questions.question: What is Metro 2039 about?
questions.answer: Metro 2039 continues Metro's post-apocalyptic saga with a war-shaped, anti-tyranny story anchored in survival, politics, and human cost.

questions.question: Which Metro 2039 platforms are being tracked?
questions.answer: Metro 2039 platform tracking centers on PC, Xbox Series X|S, and PlayStation 5 alongside storefront and localization coverage.

questions.question: Which Metro 2039 games should I play first?
questions.answer: Play Metro 2033 Redux, Metro: Last Light Redux, and Metro Exodus in order before Metro 2039 for the strongest catch-up route.

questions.question: Which Metro 2039 books should I read first?
questions.answer: Read Metro 2033, Metro 2034, and Metro 2035 in that order to build the book-side context behind Metro 2039.

cta.title: Follow Metro 2039 Official Channels
cta.description: Watch the official reveal, join the Metro community on Discord, and keep up with fresh Metro 2039 updates as Deep Silver and 4A Games share more details.
